posts.author: rdmotwani (Ravi Motwani)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone,

I am trying to create automatic report export from monday.com.

Scenario!
I have to extract and send reporting based on a board which has say about 25 columns. But the reports needed should have only 6-7 columns. I was successfully about to create a view based on the required structure but then i realized that monday.com doesn’t allow excel export based on specific view but only the main table (rolls eyes).

So another solution is to create a board which will have all the report related columns only and somehow connect the items from the main board so that the columns can be updated automatically.

I am not sure how to achieve it using connected boards or automations. Any help here would be appreciated.

Regards,
Ravi

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
@rdmotwani if an add-on is an option, I suggest taking a look at the Analytics & Reports by Screenful. You can use it to create reports of your Monday data share them via email or Slack as a PDF on a custom schedule. You can learn more from the Tour page.

Here’s a setup guide to get started with the free trial.

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Ravi ,

I’m not sure if it is still relevant to you, but I came across your post and wanted to share it. Recently, we had a similar case from a client – they asked to make custom board snapshots in xls📷. I’ve described it here. I hope it will be helpful.

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @rdmotwani The cleanest solution is a dedicated Reporting Board.
Create a new board with only the 6 to 7 columns you need.

Connect it to your main board using a Connect Boards column.
Mirror only the specific columns required for reporting.
These stay automatically in sync and export cleanly to Excel

Helpful links
https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360001276465-Connect-Boards-Column
https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360001259169-Mirror-Column

If you need more control you can also use automations to create and update items in a reporting board with only selected fields.

https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360002197259-Automations

Hope this helps and happy building

Dr. Tanvi Sachar
Monday Certified Partner, Monday Wizard

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @rdmotwani ,

You can try the Mirror Item Multiple Boards app.

It allows you to mirror multiple items from different boards into one board with full live sync.

You can create a master board with tasks from different board.

Then you can export the master board to generate your report.

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @rdmotwani, one practical workaround (without duplicating boards or building complex mirror setups) is using Time in Status for monday.com. It lets you generate clean, purpose-built reports with only the columns and metrics you actually need, and export them separately from the main board structure. This avoids maintaining a second “reporting board” just for exports.

It’s especially useful if your reports are recurring and need to be shared externally or with leadership.

There’s a 14-day free trial, and you can also schedule a demo to walk through how to structure the reports exactly the way you need before committing.
